notes: Wikipedia stored_url entity-matches exactly (Japanese jazz fusion guitarist, Feb 25 1947 - Apr 13 2020). Every bio claim confirmed verbatim: 'one of the first musicians to develop and popularise the fusion genre', guitar synthesizer with Roland Corporation and Korg, Kawasaki Synthesizer for the Commodore 64, died April 13 2020. birthYear 1947, deathYear 2020, instruments guitar+synthesizer all consistent.

ours.wikipediaUrl already points to the correctly disambiguated page en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sadao_Watanabe_(musician); websearch resolved=true confirms it is the jazz saxophonist (born 1 Feb 1933, living, alto+sopranino sax, bossa nova/crossover). The stored evidence.wikipedia is the plain-title disambiguation page (artist 1913-1996 vs musician), not what the site ships. birthYear 1933 confirmed. Minor nuance: websearch lead names 'alto saxophone and sopranino saxophone' while ours lists alto/soprano/flute; the lead is not an exhaustive instrument denial, so absence of soprano/flute is not a contradiction (not flagged). Brazilian/African exploration consistent with crossover/bossa-nova note.

ours.wikipediaUrl points to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sam_Rivers_(jazz_musician); websearch resolved=true confirms (Samuel Carthorne Rivers, Sep 25 1923 - Dec 26 2011, founder of Studio Rivbea). Stored evidence.wikipedia is the plain-title disambiguation page (jazz musician vs Limp Bizkit bassist). birthYear 1923, deathYear 2011, instruments (tenor/soprano sax, flute, piano) all match websearch findings (which also list bass clarinet). Studio Rivbea claim confirmed.

Wikipedia stored_url entity-matches exactly (Walter Theodore 'Sonny' Rollins, Sep 7 1930 - May 25 2026). All checkable bio claims confirmed verbatim: tenor saxophonist, 'the greatest living improviser', 1956 Saxophone Colossus, 'St. Thomas' calypso signature, Coleman Hawkins his idol, died May 2026, 'recorded more than 60 albums as a leader'. birthYear 1930, deathYear 2026 match. The Williamsburg Bridge sabbatical detail is not in this extract excerpt but is not contradicted (absence != contradiction).

Wikipedia stored_url entity-matches exactly (British jazz band, short desc '2011-2022'). Bio confirmed: led by Shabaka Hutchings (saxophone+clarinet), tuba and two drummers, mix of jazz/rock/Caribbean folk/African music, politically charged (Your Queen Is a Reptile), disbanded 2022. birthYear 2011 matches short-description/founding; instruments saxophone/tuba/drums consistent.

Wikipedia stored_url entity-matches exactly (Le Sony'r Ra, born Herman Poole Blount, May 22 1914 - May 30 1993). Bio confirmed: claimed to be an alien from Saturn, led The Arkestra from the mid-1950s until his death, pioneer of Afrofuturism, music spanned swing/bebop/free jazz/electronic, Egyptian-inspired costumes. birthYear 1914, deathYear 1993 match; piano+synthesizer confirmed (organ not named but not contradicted).

Wikipedia stored_url entity-matches exactly (Terence Oliver Blanchard, born Mar 13 1962). Bio confirmed: trumpeter/composer, joined Art Blakey's Jazz Messengers in 1982 as Wynton Marsalis's replacement (both New Orleans), composed score for most Spike Lee films, A Tale of God's Will (Requiem for Katrina), Fire Shut Up in My Bones first opera by a Black composer at the Metropolitan Opera (2021-22 season). birthYear 1962 matches; instruments trumpet/composer consistent.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: The World Saxophone Quartet was an American jazz ensemble founded in 1977

notes: Right entity (Wikipedia stored_url matches: original members Julius Hemphill, Oliver Lake, Hamiet Bluiett, David Murray; saxophone quartet, no rhythm section). birthYear FIELD = 1976 contradicts evidence 'founded in 1977' (the bio prose itself correctly says 'Founded in 1977', so field is internally inconsistent and wrong). The 'Revue' album reference is unverifiable (no discography/tracklist in evidence) but not flaggable. Repair: set birthYear to 1977.

ours.wikipediaUrl is already null (correct). Stored ja.wikipedia URL (有馬祐輝) returns 404/page=null and the kanji may not be his name; no Wikipedia exists in any language. websearch resolved=true confirms the artist (Japanese jazz pianist, born 1961 Tokyo per HighResAudio) and ours.birthYear 1961 matches; instruments piano confirmed. No contradiction. Two substantive bio claims are uncheckable against available evidence: 'Bill Evans's influence' and 'His Village Vanguard appearance' are not corroborated by any fetched source (not provably false either). Hence unverifiable, not factual_error.
